title: Fixing the React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object Reference

statement.markdown: You are given a React memoized component that is intended to optimize performance by minimizing unnecessary re-renders. However, the component is currently re-rendering when the object properties change, rather than when the object reference changes. Fix the bug to make the component only re-render when the object reference changes.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an object with key-value pairs, where each key is a string and each value is an object.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the updated object with the correct React memoization behavior.
statement.notes: The component should not re-render when the object properties change, but should re-render when the object reference changes.

constraints.description: The number of key-value pairs in the input object.
The number of key-value pairs in the input object.

examples.explanation: The object reference has not changed, so the component should not re-render.
The object properties have changed, but the object reference has not changed, so the component should not re-render.
The object reference has changed, so the component should re-render.

hints.text: Use the `useCallback` hook to memoize the function that depends on the object reference.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing the React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object Reference
editorial.intuition: To fix this bug, we need to use React's memoization features to ensure that the component only re-renders when the object reference changes. We can use the `useCallback` hook to memoize the function that depends on the object reference.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible solution is to use a library like `lodash` to create a deep copy of the object and compare the references.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use React's built-in memoization features.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the `useCallback` hook to memoize the function that depends on the object reference.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: To prove the correctness of this solution, we can use a debugger or a testing library to verify that the component only re-renders when the object reference changes.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of this solution would involve creating a simple React component that uses the `useCallback` hook to memoize a function that depends on an object reference, and then testing it with different inputs to verify that it only re-renders when the object reference changes.

starterCode.python: class Solution: def use_object_reference(self, obj) { /* implementation */ }

buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def use_object_reference(self, obj) { return str(obj); }

functionSignature.python: class Solution: def use_object_reference(self, obj) { /* implementation */ }

solutions.javascript:
// Create a new function that depends on the object reference
var useObjectReference = useCallback(() => JSON.stringify(obj), [obj]);
